column1: # prerename-leak-verdicts.tsv — single source of truth for the per-path
# migrate-vs-allowlist VERDICT TABLE the temperloop#165 foundation->temperloop
# #433, gate-sweep item) so a pre-rename identifier can migrate/allowlist
# ONLY per an explicit, reviewed row here — never by a silent new occurrence.
#              path segment of a `foundation/<leaf>` XDG-rooted machine-state
#              subdir, matched by the guard's `foundation/<leaf>` scan).
#   token    — exact identifier (env) or exact leaf segment (path-leaf).
#              literal — legitimate ONLY through the v0.17.0 removal window;
#              this row is safe to delete once that window closes and the
#              legacy read is removed), "allowlist" (a grandfathered path the
#              rename item deliberately did NOT migrate — no removal date,
#              stays until a later coordinated-move item revisits it), or
#              "no-action" (matches the FOUNDATION_ shape but is not a
#              stranger-facing config surface at all — an internal
#              script/test-local variable name; nothing to migrate or
#              allowlist, listed defensively so it doesn't read as an
#   rationale — why, citing the verdict-table row it encodes (PR #487 Sec.
#              paragraph, so a fix is obvious without re-deriving it here.
# NOT covered here (verdict "no-action", no row needed — no FOUNDATION_-shaped
# or foundation/-shaped literal exists for either): KS_LIB_DIR (name-neutral;
# claude/hooks/*'s BASH_SOURCE-relative climb has no foundation-named default
# The compat shim's OWN two remaining intentional legacy literal FORMS —
# "Committed .foundation/ per-repo dir") and the CLI compat shim's own binary
# path (`bin/foundation`) — are NOT enumerated here as rows: they are always
# sanctioned by check-prerename-leak-guard.sh's own pattern logic (matched
# unconditionally, no per-leaf lookup), since the rename item's whole point
# was to keep EVERY leaf under the legacy dir, and the shim binary itself,
# working through the window — see that script's header for the two

column4: Env-var prefix migration (temperloop#165): canonical TEMPERLOOP_HOME since v0.15.0; FOUNDATION_HOME still read as fallback (bin/bootstrap.sh, bin/lib/common.sh, knob-registry.tsv); legacy read removed in v0.17.0 — delete this row at window close.
Env-var prefix migration: canonical TEMPERLOOP_BIN_DIR since v0.15.0; legacy read removed in v0.17.0 — delete this row at window close.
Env-var prefix migration: canonical TEMPERLOOP_KERNEL_REPO since v0.15.0; legacy read removed in v0.17.0 — delete this row at window close.
Env-var prefix migration: canonical TEMPERLOOP_VERSION since v0.15.0 (bin/subcommands/feedback.sh, bin/temperloop); legacy read removed in v0.17.0 — delete this row at window close.

bin/lib/common.sh's internal constant name mirroring FOUNDATION_HOME's old default value (bootstrap hand-synced triplication site, acceptance 2); removed in v0.17.0 alongside the rest of the compat shim — delete this row at window close.

bin/lib/common.sh's internal constant name mirroring FOUNDATION_BIN_DIR's old default value (bootstrap hand-synced triplication site, acceptance 2); removed in v0.17.0 — delete this row at window close.
bin/subcommands/tests/test_report_offer.sh's own test-local shell variable naming the path to its bin/foundation shim fixture; a test's internal naming choice, not a stranger-facing config knob.
workflows/scripts/kernel/tests/test_check_producer_egress.sh's own test-local shell variable naming a directory one level up in that test's scratch layout; not a stranger-facing config knob.

workflows/scripts/install/links.sh's own private include-guard variable (`_FOUNDATION_LINKS_SH_LOADED`, matched without its leading underscore) preventing double-sourcing; never read or set by anything outside this one file, not a stranger-facing config knob.

BOARDS_CONF_MACHINE (PR #487 verdict table): migrate-with-read-old — new default $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/temperloop/boards.conf; existing legacy $XDG_CONFIG_HOME/foundation/boards.conf read as fallback at all 7 reader sites; legacy read removed in v0.17.0 — delete this row at window close.

KNOWLEDGE_STORE_ROOT (PR #487 verdict table): migrate-with-read-old — new default .../temperloop/knowledge; an existing legacy .../foundation/knowledge store is still found through the window; legacy fallback removed in v0.17.0 — delete this row at window close.

ENV_RECONCILE_AGENT_HEARTBEAT_DIR (PR #487 verdict table): allowlist, no migration this PR — launchd heartbeat writers and env-reconcile.sh's freshness-oracle reader update on different cadences; a reader-only migration would silently blind the oracle. No removal date; revisit only via a coordinated writer+reader move.

KNOWLEDGE_READ_LOG: part of the ${XDG_STATE_HOME}/foundation/ machine-state family (CHANGELOG "Grandfathered machine-state paths" paragraph) — installed hook copies (~/.claude/hooks) write on make-install cadence, not repo cadence; a per-site move would split read-log tallies across two dirs.
KNOWLEDGE_SEARCH_BM_HOME: same machine-state family as knowledge-reads.log above; same split-state hazard.
EVAL_DENIAL_LOG: same machine-state family; same split-state hazard.
session-start-drain.sh's own failure log: same machine-state family; same split-state hazard.
bin/temperloop's report-offer dismissal marker dir: same machine-state family; same split-state hazard.

install-gitleaks.sh's local tool-cache dir (${XDG_CACHE_HOME}/foundation/gitleaks/$GITLEAKS_VERSION) — not enumerated in the original PR #487 verdict table (a disposable re-downloadable cache, not a persisted contract surface); allowlisted here defensively under the same "no coordinated-move benefit yet" rationale as the machine-state family, pending a future explicit no-action confirmation.
